mission.title: Install and Configure Spinkube on Kubernetes
mission.description: Spin Operator is a Kubernetes operator that enables platform engineers to deploy Spin applications as custom resources to their Kubernetes clusters. Installing it allows you to manage Spin applications efficiently within your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Spin Operator Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add spin-operator https://github.com/spinframework/spin-operator/releases/download/v0.6.1
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Spin Operator
steps.description:
Install the Spin Operator using Helm in a dedicated namespace.
```bash
helm install spin-operator spin-operator/spin-operator --namespace spin-operator --create-namespace --version 0.6.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Spin Operator pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spin-operator
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Spin Operator deployment to set resource limits for the operator container.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment spin-operator-controller-manager --namespace spin-operator --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "128Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "10m", "memory": "64Mi"}}}]'
```

steps.title: Deploy a sample Spin application
steps.description:
Apply a sample Spin application configuration to test the operator.
```bash
kubectl apply -f ./config/samples/simple.yaml --namespace spin-operator
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the application
steps.description:
Forward a local port to access the deployed Spin application.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/simple-spinapp 8083:80 --namespace spin-operator
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Spin Operator Helm release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall spin-operator --namespace spin-operator
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Custom Resource Definitions
uninstall.description:
Delete the SpinApp and SpinAppExecutor CRDs from the cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete crd spinapps.core.spinkube.dev
kubectl delete crd spinappexecutors.core.spinkube.dev
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for the Spin Operator.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace spin-operator
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and resources for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace spin-operator -o yaml > backup-spin-operator.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Fetch the latest Helm chart updates.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Spin Operator
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Spin Operator to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade spin-operator spin-operator/spin-operator --namespace spin-operator --version 0.6.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Spin Operator pods are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spin-operator
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the Spin Operator pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace spin-operator
```

troubleshooting.title: Spin application not responding
troubleshooting.description:
If the Spin application is not responding, verify the service and pod status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace spin-operator
kubectl get pods --namespace spin-operator
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release failed
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm release fails, check the Helm release history for errors.
```bash
helm history spin-operator --namespace spin-operator
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors related to CRDs, ensure they are installed correctly.
```bash
kubectl get crd --namespace spin-operator
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of the Spin Operator will show the operator pods running in the 'spin-operator' namespace. You should also be able to access the sample Spin application via the port-forwarded local address.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add spin-operator https://github.com/spinframework/spin-operator/releases/download/v0.6.1
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure that Helm and kubectl are installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
